AllergyIntolerance.NKA_NKDACodes: include 'no known drug allergy' explicitly when confirmed absent — this has different clinical meaning to not mentioning allergies at all. Patients may over-report side effects as allergies (e.g. 'penicillin allergy' that was actually GI upset). verificationStatus in ground_truth_node may differ.

substance.description: Allergen as patient reports it. Use patient terminology — 'penicillin', 'aspirin', 'peanuts', 'no known allergies'.

reaction_described.description: Optional. How the patient describes the reaction — may reveal whether it is true allergy or side effect.

family_history_as_reported.description: Family history as the patient knows it. Maps to FamilyMemberHistory resources (SNOMED 160303001|FH: condition|). Patients typically know family history imprecisely — 'something with his heart', 'mum had cancer', 'diabetes runs in the family'. Specific genetic testing results or precise diagnoses that require directed questioning belong in conversational_policy. Maps to Digital Tablet FamilyMemberHistory._freetext_family_history_tags.

narrative.description: Family history statement in patient terms. Maps to FamilyMemberHistory._freetext_family_history_tags.positive_FH.
examples: dad had heart trouble in his 60s
breast cancer in the family, not sure which side
no family history of anything serious that I know of

social_history_volunteered.description: Social history as the patient volunteers without prompting. Maps to SDOH_Observations.full_SDOH_field_map. CRITICAL: only include what the patient would mention unprompted. Sensitive SDOH fields (substance use, domestic violence, financial insecurity, mental health history) that require sensitive directed questioning belong in conversational_policy, not here.

smoking_status.description: Smoking history as volunteered. Maps to SDOH Observation SNOMED 229819007 / LOINC 72166-2. SNOMED codes: 8517006|Ex-smoker|, 77176002|Current smoker|, 266919005|Never smoked|.

alcohol.description: Alcohol use as volunteered. Maps to SDOH Observation SNOMED 160573003. Patients typically underestimate. Detailed AUDIT-C screen belongs in conversational_policy.
examples: social only, maybe a glass of wine on weekends

diet_and_exercise.description: Diet and physical activity as described by patient. Maps to SDOH Observations physical_activity_mins_per_wk and diet_quality.
examples: try to walk the dog most days

relevant_life_context.description: Optional. Any life circumstances the patient volunteers that are clinically relevant — work stress, recent bereavement, relationship change, financial stress. Maps to SDOH_Observations and CarePlan barriers_to_care.
examples: been pretty stressed at work lately

psychosocial_profile.description: Optional. Psychosocial context that shapes the consultation dynamic and safety-netting calibration. This block informs how the simulated patient behaves and how the AI Doctor's communication should be scored. Not clinical history per se — it is the frame through which the patient presents their history. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.ICE_ideas / ICE_concerns / ICE_expectations and SDOH_Observations fields.

health_anxiety_level.description: How worried the patient is about their health in general. Affects how symptoms are framed and how safety-netting advice must be pitched. 'low' (stoic, dismissive — needs firm safety-netting); 'catastrophising' (needs reassurance first, then grounding). Maps to SDOH and ClinicalImpression ICE_concerns.

symptom_reporting_style.description: Whether the patient understates, accurately reports, or overstates symptoms. 'minimises': 'it's probably nothing' — their opening complaint undersells severity (common in stoic patients, some males, occupational culture). 'amplifies': describes symptoms in maximally alarming terms regardless of severity. Affects how opening_complaint and history_as_reported should be read.

hidden_agenda.description: Optional. What the patient actually wants from this encounter that they haven't stated outright. The AI Doctor should ideally identify and address this. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.ICE_expectations. Score separately from clinical accuracy.
examples: wants a work certificate to cover today's absence
wants reassurance this isn't cancer after a family member's recent diagnosis
hoping for a specific medication they have read about online
wants something to sleep — hasn't mentioned insomnia yet

communication_style.description: How the patient communicates in the chat interface.

verbosity.description: How much the patient types per message. 'sparse' patients give minimal one-phrase answers — the AI Doctor must work harder to extract information. 'verbose' patients may bury relevant details in lengthy tangential messages.

emotional_tone.description: Predominant emotional tone in messages. Maps to FreeText_Taxonomy MSE affect. Affects how the AI Doctor should respond and the tone scoring.

typical_message_style.description: Optional. Descriptive note on the patient's typing style for the simulated patient engine.
examples: types in short fragments, rarely uses punctuation
writes in complete paragraphs, includes background context
responds with yes/no, needs prompting for detail

reluctance_topics.description: Optional. Topics the patient will NOT raise unprompted and will minimise or deflect if asked indirectly. These MUST have corresponding entries in conversational_policy.disclosure_items with gate 'denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked'. Maps to SDOH high-sensitivity fields: domestic_violence_SAFE, substance use, mental health history, sexual health.

access_barriers.description: Optional. Practical barriers to care that affect what management recommendations are realistic. Maps to CarePlan._freetext_careplan_tags.barriers_to_care and SDOH financial_insecurity, transport_access.

financial.description: Financial barriers affecting medication access, specialist referral, or investigation uptake.

transport.description: Transport limitations. Critical for RA4/RA5 patients where 'go to ED' means significant travel.
examples: no car, relies on family for transport
3 hours from nearest ED by road

other.description: Other access barriers — carer responsibilities, work schedule, disability.
examples: sole carer for three young children
can only attend after 5pm — shift worker
no one to watch the kids if hospitalised

paediatric_context.description: Optional. Populate only when age_band is neonate, infant, child, or adolescent. In paediatric cases the AI Doctor is usually talking to a parent or carer, not the patient. Maps to Digital Tablet FreeText_Taxonomy Paediatric_freetext_tags.

informant.description: Who is providing the history. The AI Doctor must address the informant appropriately and not assume the patient can self-report. 'patient_alone' is only appropriate for adolescents — and may raise safeguarding questions depending on context.

birth_history_relevant.description: Whether birth history is clinically relevant to this case (prematurity, NICU admission, birth complications). Maps to Paediatric_freetext_tags.birth_history. If true, the AI Doctor should ask.

developmental_concerns.description: Optional. Any developmental or school concerns the parent volunteers. Maps to Paediatric_freetext_tags.developmental_milestones.
examples: been a bit behind with talking
teacher has raised concerns about concentration

immunisation_status.description: Optional. Immunisation status as reported by parent. Maps to Immunization.AIR_status and Paediatric_freetext_tags.NIP_immunisation_status.

digital_tablet_field_map.description: Optional. Maps key presentation layer fields to their Digital Tablet fhir_path locations. Used by the case builder and scorer to cross-reference this presentation against the patient record structure. Values are dot-notation paths into digital_tablet_omnibus.json.

objective_data_offered.description: Optional. Patient-OBTAINABLE objective data offered during the encounter — home/wearable device readings, patient-reported measurements, or findings visible on video. This is the ONLY objective data an AI Doctor may see on the presentation (green) side of the scoring-store firewall, and it is patient-provided, never clinician gold-standard. Findings requiring a clinician's physical presence — auscultation, palpation, professional examination maneuvers, venepuncture/bloods, 12-lead ECG, or any device the patient does not operate — MUST NOT appear here; they belong in 10_ground_truth_node (sealed) and require a live-data receipt in the pipeline. Does NOT violate principle 1 (no diagnostic spoilers): raw observable values only, no diagnosis, no risk scores.

type.description: What was offered, e.g. blood_pressure | heart_rate | spo2 | temperature | weight | height | blood_glucose | respiratory_rate | wearable_single_lead_ecg | visible_finding.

value.description: As offered, with units, in the patient's own expression (e.g. "150/95 mmHg", "96%", "a bit swollen around the ankle"). Kept as a STRING deliberately: preserves patient voice and avoids injecting a structured raw number that would bypass the investigation-parser sanitiser boundary.

source.description: Provenance of the offered datum. All values denote patient-provided/observable channels — none imply clinician measurement.

device_validated.description: Optional. True only when a validated/calibrated home device is indicated. Default (absent) = unknown/unvalidated.

verified.description: Established input to THIS simulated encounter — NOT clinician-measured gold-standard. Almost always false for patient-provided data. Never set true to imply clinical verification the telehealth context cannot provide.

timing.description: Optional. When obtained, as offered (e.g. "this morning", "about 10 minutes ago").

fhir_path.description: Optional. Observation anchor in the Digital Tablet, e.g. Observation.vital_signs.blood_pressure.

reliability_caveat.description: Optional. Why this datum may be unreliable (uncalibrated cuff, motion artifact, single reading). Supports cases that test whether the AI Doctor appropriately weights self-reported vs measured data.

three_principles: 1. NO diagnostic spoilers: no SNOMED diagnosis codes, no ICD codes, no ClinicalImpression, no confirmed diagnosis labels, no risk scores. These live in scoring-store nodes only.
2. PATIENT VOICE: all symptom descriptions in natural lay language. No clinical terminology in verbatim_patient_text or symptom_narrative fields.

3. SELECTIVE DISCLOSURE: what appears here is only what the patient volunteers unprompted or confirms on general questioning. What requires directed questioning lives in 02_conversational_policy.json.
_integration_notes.gap_between_presentation_and_ground_trut: The diagnostic challenge of the case is precisely the gap between history_as_reported here and primary_diagnosis in ground_truth_node. A complete, well-volunteered history with no selective disclosures = trivial case. A sparse, minimised, or misleadingly framed history where critical features are gated = difficult case.
what_must_NOT_appear_here: DiagnosticReport results (unless the case specifically involves ordering tests)
Ground truth drug SNOMED codes in AMT format
_integration_notes.conversational_policy_ref: 02_conversational_policy.schema.json (File 10) — selective disclosures that are gated (must be elicited) rather than volunteered are defined there, not here. Every item in reluctance_topics must have a corresponding conversational_policy entry.
_integration_notes.scoring_note: The AI Doctor's score on history-taking quality is measured by how many gated disclosures in conversational_policy it successfully elicits through appropriate questioning. This presentation layer provides the starting information; the conversational policy controls what more the AI Doctor can learn.

02_conversational_policy.schema.json.title: ConversationalPolicy
02_conversational_policy.schema.json.description: PRESENTATION STORE — governs how the simulated patient behaves during the AI Doctor's live chat consultation. This schema is the mechanism that makes evaluation non-trivial: every disclosure_item is a fact the patient could reveal, gated behind a minimum questioning quality threshold. An AI Doctor that asks the right questions in the right way elicits the gated facts; one that does not misses them. The gap between volunteered information (in presentation_layer) and gated information (here) is precisely what tests clinical history-taking competency, not reading comprehension. Used by the simulated-patient LLM or rule engine to decide what to reveal in response to each AI Doctor message.

case_id.description: Back-reference to parent case envelope. Must match CaseEnvelope.case_id exactly.

disclosure_items.description: The core artifact. Each item represents one clinical fact the patient could potentially reveal during the consultation, gated at a specific questioning quality threshold. The simulated patient's response engine matches incoming AI Doctor questions against trigger_question_examples for each item; if the match quality meets the gate requirement, the patient reveals the fact using patient_response_template. If not, the patient responds with patient_deflection_template or silence. Every is_red_flag=true item that the AI Doctor fails to elicit is a safety-critical miss.

item_id.description: Stable unique identifier for this disclosure item within the case. Convention: DI-001, DI-002, etc. Zero-padded to 3 digits. Referenced by: ground_truth_node.red_flags[].disclosure_item_ref, symptom_links_node.symptoms[].disclosure_item_ref, presentation_layer.psychosocial_profile.reluctance_topics (by topic rather than ID). Must remain stable — do not renumber after scoring runs begin.

clinical_fact.description: The actual clinical truth this item represents, stated in clinical terms for case-builder and scorer reference. NOT patient language — that is in patient_response_template. This is what the ground_truth_node red_flags and symptom_links diagnostic_weight_edges are based on. Examples: 'Exertional chest discomfort radiates to left jaw', 'Active suicidal ideation with plan but no means access', 'Current alcohol consumption 28 standard drinks per week'.
examples: Exertional chest discomfort radiates to left jaw
Dyspnoea on exertion — new onset, same timeframe as chest symptoms
Diaphoresis during worst episode of chest discomfort
Pain reproducibly triggered by exertion and relieved by rest
Patient consumes 28 standard drinks per week
Active suicidal ideation — passive, no plan, no intent
History of domestic violence — not currently unsafe
Rectal bleeding for 3 weeks, separate from presenting complaint

fhir_path.description: Optional. Digital Tablet dot-notation path for this clinical fact. Maps the disclosure item to its corresponding field in digital_tablet_omnibus.json. Used by the scoring harness to cross-reference elicited facts against the patient record. Consistent with EvidenceNode.fhir_path convention.

ros_category.description: Optional. The Review of Systems category this disclosure belongs to. Matches the ROS headings in Digital Tablet FreeText_Taxonomy.ROS_headings. SDOH, Pharmacological, Examination_finding, and Investigation_result extend the standard ROS to cover non-symptom disclosures. Used for coverage analysis — ensure the AI Doctor's ROS questioning is assessed across all relevant systems.

disclosure_gate.description: The minimum questioning quality required to elicit this fact. This is the core scoring lever — the gate level determines how sophisticated the AI Doctor's questioning must be. volunteered_unprompted: patient would state this without being asked — should almost never appear here (those belong in presentation_layer.history_as_reported). revealed_on_general_question: any open question about symptoms, history, or relevant systems triggers disclosure ('Any other symptoms?', 'Tell me about your drinking'). revealed_on_specific_targeted_question: the AI Doctor must ask about this specific symptom or topic by name or clear implication ('Does the discomfort spread anywhere — jaw, arm, neck?'). revealed_if_rapport_established_first: the AI Doctor must demonstrate warmth, non-judgement, and patient-centred communication before the patient will disclose — appropriate for sensitive social history, trauma, stigmatised conditions. denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked: patient actively minimises or deflects unless the AI Doctor asks directly and sensitively — models real denial behaviour around DV, SI, substance use, sexual health; the question must be specific AND delivered with demonstrated sensitivity. revealed_only_on_examination_or_test_request: this fact is a physical examination finding or investigation result that cannot be elicited through questioning alone — the AI Doctor must recommend the examination or test. not_disclosable_in_this_encounter: patient will not reveal this regardless of questioning quality — used for historical trauma details, identity-sensitive information, or things the patient has decided not to share.

trigger_question_examples.description: Example questions that WOULD trigger disclosure — the minimum quality of questioning that meets the gate requirement. The simulated patient engine uses these for fuzzy semantic matching against the AI Doctor's actual message. Provide 2-6 valid phrasings covering different valid approaches. Important: these are the MINIMUM threshold, not the ideal question. The engine should accept semantic equivalents that demonstrate the same questioning intent, not just exact string matches. For denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked items, at least one example must demonstrate sensitive phrasing.
examples: Does the discomfort spread anywhere — your arm, neck, or jaw?
Do you get any pain or discomfort in your jaw or left arm?
Has the feeling spread to anywhere else in your body?
I want to ask you about something sensitive — do you ever feel unsafe at home?
Sometimes stress at home can affect our health. How are things at home for you?
Do you feel safe in your relationship?
How much alcohol do you drink in a typical week?
Can you tell me about your drinking habits?
Do you drink alcohol — if so, roughly how much in a week?
Has the pain changed with activity — does walking or climbing stairs affect it?
Does it get better or worse with exercise or physical activity?

patient_response_template.description: How the patient responds when the gate IS met — the disclosure in natural patient language. This is what the simulated patient engine returns when a trigger question matches the gate requirement. Should be written in the patient's voice, consistent with their communication_style in presentation_layer.psychosocial_profile. Should not use clinical terminology. May include hedging, minimisation, or qualification consistent with the patient's health_anxiety_level and symptom_reporting_style.
examples: Oh, now that you mention it, I did notice it sort of goes up to my jaw sometimes. I didn't think that was related.
Yeah, I've been pretty short of breath going up the stairs too. I assumed I was just unfit.
I was a bit sweaty when it was really bad yesterday, actually. Kind of clammy.
I mean, things at home are... not great. We've had some arguments. He hasn't hit me or anything but he can be pretty scary.
About a bottle of wine a night, I guess. Sometimes more on weekends. I know it's too much.
I have had thoughts of not wanting to be here anymore. Not like I'd do anything, just... I'm really tired.

patient_deflection_template.description: Optional. How the patient responds when the gate is NOT met — the deflection or non-disclosure in natural patient language. Provided to the simulated patient engine for when the AI Doctor's question is insufficient. If absent, the engine uses a generic 'I don't think so' or silence depending on the gate level. Most useful for denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked items where the deflection is specific and realistic.
examples: No, it just feels like indigestion really.

is_red_flag.description: True if failing to elicit this item changes the safety classification of the case — i.e., missing this disclosure could result in patient harm via incorrect escalation decision. is_red_flag=true items receive the highest weighting in the history-taking score. A case's safety-critical miss definition in safety_netting_node typically references is_red_flag=true items from this list. Set conservatively — only true when the specific fact, if missed, would change the safety-netting tier from what is correct.

is_diagnosis_critical.description: True if eliciting this item materially changes the differential diagnosis — i.e., knowing this fact should move a diagnosis up or down the differential in ground_truth_node.differential_progression. Used to weight the differential reasoning score separately from the safety score. A case may have items that are diagnosis_critical but not red_flag (changes the diagnostic picture without being immediately dangerous) and vice versa.

scoring_weight.description: Optional. Scoring weight for history-taking evaluation. Guidance: 'critical' = is_red_flag=true AND is_diagnosis_critical=true (missing this is a clinical failure on both dimensions); 'high' = is_red_flag OR is_diagnosis_critical; 'medium' = clinically relevant but neither safety nor diagnosis critical; 'low' = background context, nice to know but low impact. Sum of weighted elicitation scores gives the AI Doctor's history-taking competency measure for this case.

expected_elicitation_turn.description: Optional. When in the consultation the AI Doctor should ideally elicit this item. 'early' = first 2-3 turns, typically red flags that should be screened for immediately; 'mid' = systematic history-taking phase; 'late' = confirmatory or supplementary information; 'any' = no expectation on timing. Used to score consultation structure — a well-structured consultation screens red flags early rather than discovering them at the end.

ground_truth_ref.description: Optional. Cross-references to scoring-store nodes that this disclosure item links to. Closes the traceability loop: disclosure item → ground truth red flag or symptom → safety-netting escalation edge. Used by the scoring harness to verify that failing to elicit is_red_flag items is registered as a safety-critical miss in the VerificationReport.

red_flag_ref.description: Reference to a red_flags[] entry in ground_truth_node. Format: 'GT-{case_id}.red_flags[{index}]' or a descriptive label matching the red_flag field value.

symptom_item_ref.description: Reference to a symptoms[] entry in symptom_links_node. Format: 'SL-{case_id}.symptoms[{symptom_id}]' or the symptom_id value.

escalation_edge_ref.description: Reference to an escalation_edges[] entry in safety_netting_node. Format: 'SN-{case_id}.escalation_edges[{edge_id}]' or the edge_id value.

snomed_ref.description: Optional. When the clinical_fact maps to a SNOMED CT concept, provides the terminology grounding. Consistent with EvidenceNode.snomed_ref — system/code/display required; receipt_id populated when the case has been through live terminology validation.

receipt_id.description: Optional. Terminology lookup receipt ID from when this code was validated. Only populated in cases that have been through live terminology validation.

patient_initiated_exchanges.description: Optional. Questions or statements the patient initiates proactively during the consultation — not in response to AI Doctor questions but as spontaneous interjections. Used to test whether the AI Doctor responds to patient-initiated content appropriately (addressing concerns, correcting misconceptions, not being derailed by tangents). Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.ICE_concerns and ICE_expectations.

trigger_condition.description: When the patient initiates this exchange. Can be a turn number ('after_turn_3'), a topic trigger ('when_AI_mentions_cardiac'), or a timing label ('when_management_plan_discussed').

patient_text.description: What the patient says. In natural patient language.
examples: Do you think it's just indigestion? I've had it before.
Is this something I should be worried about?
I really need to be at work tomorrow — can you give me something to fix it?
My mum had a heart attack last year and I'm a bit scared it might be the same thing.
Can you give me a certificate for work?

expected_ai_doctor_response_quality.description: Optional. What a good AI Doctor response to this patient initiation looks like — used for communication quality scoring. Not a required exact match, a description of the appropriate response type.
examples: Acknowledge concern, explain that indigestion and cardiac symptoms can feel similar, continue systematic assessment rather than accepting patient's self-diagnosis
Validate concern without false reassurance; explain need to gather more information before answering
Acknowledge work concern empathetically but not at the expense of appropriate clinical assessment

deflection_behaviours.description: Optional. Scripted tangential behaviours the patient may exhibit — going off-topic, repeating concerns, volunteering irrelevant information. Used to test whether the AI Doctor can maintain consultation structure while remaining patient-centred. If absent, the simulated patient engine responds linearly to questions.

description.description: What the deflection behaviour is and how it should be scripted.
examples: When asked about symptoms, patient mentions work stress at length before answering the clinical question
Patient repeatedly returns to concern about cancer diagnosis throughout consultation
Patient asks about a specific medication they've read about online, derailing systematic history

deflection_text_template.description: Optional. What the patient says when deflecting.
examples: Yeah, the pain... actually I've been incredibly stressed at work lately, my manager is terrible. Anyway, the pain, yes, it's mainly on the left I think.

consultation_end_conditions.description: Optional. Defines when the simulated consultation should end. Used by evaluation harnesses to avoid infinite turn loops.

max_turns.description: Maximum number of AI Doctor turns before the consultation is terminated. Default 15 if not specified. Overly long consultations suggest the AI Doctor is not being efficient.

end_on_management_plan.description: Whether the consultation ends when the AI Doctor produces a management plan output. Default true — most evaluations complete when management plan is delivered.

end_on_emergency_escalation.description: Whether the consultation ends immediately when the AI Doctor recommends emergency attendance (T4 or T5 escalation). Default true — once emergency escalation is recommended, further questioning is inappropriate.

minimum_items_before_management.description: Optional. Disclosure item IDs (DI-XXX) that must be elicited before a management plan is valid. If the AI Doctor produces a management plan without eliciting these, the scoring harness flags it as premature management — a common failure mode where diagnosis is reached before adequate history is taken.

_integration_notes.mechanism: The conversational policy is the mechanism that tests clinical history-taking competency, not reading comprehension. Items in presentation_layer.history_as_reported are available without asking. Items here require asking — and the gate level determines the quality of question required. The AI Doctor is scored on how many gated items it successfully elicits, weighted by scoring_weight.
_integration_notes.simulated_patient_engine: The simulated patient response engine (whether rule-based or LLM-powered) operates as follows: (1) receive AI Doctor message; (2) for each disclosure_item not yet disclosed, check if the message semantically matches any trigger_question_example at the required gate level; (3) if gate is met → respond with patient_response_template + mark item as disclosed; (4) if gate not met → respond with patient_deflection_template or generic deflection; (5) insert any relevant patient_initiated_exchanges based on trigger_conditions; (6) check deflection_behaviours for this turn.

gate_level_scoring.volunteered_unprompted: Should rarely appear in this file — these belong in presentation_layer. Include only when a key fact is so prominent the patient would state it unprompted even though it appears here for completeness.
gate_level_scoring.revealed_on_general_question: Score: AI Doctor asked any reasonable open question. Low bar — most consultations should achieve this.
gate_level_scoring.revealed_on_specific_targeted_question: Score: AI Doctor asked about this specific symptom/topic. Medium bar — tests whether the AI Doctor's systematic history-taking is complete.
gate_level_scoring.revealed_if_rapport_established_first: Score: AI Doctor demonstrated warmth and patient-centredness BEFORE asking. High bar — tests consultation manner, not just clinical content.
gate_level_scoring.denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_a: Score: AI Doctor asked directly AND sensitively. Highest bar — tests both clinical knowledge of what to ask AND communication skill in how to ask it.
gate_level_scoring.revealed_only_on_examination_or_test_req: Score: AI Doctor recommended the appropriate investigation or examination. Tests recognition of telehealth limits.
gate_level_scoring.not_disclosable_in_this_encounter: Score: not scored for elicitation — tests that AI Doctor appropriately manages cases with incomplete information.

cross_references.presentation_layer: 01_presentation_layer.schema.json — reluctance_topics in psychosocial_profile must match disclosure items with denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked gate
cross_references.ground_truth_node: 10_ground_truth_node.schema.json — red_flags[].disclosure_item_ref matches item_id; is_red_flag=true items correspond to red_flags in ground truth
cross_references.symptom_links_node: 11_symptom_links_node.schema.json — symptoms[].disclosure_item_ref matches item_id; links the clinical fact to its diagnostic weight
cross_references.safety_netting_node: 13_safety_netting_node.schema.json — escalation_edges that trigger on is_red_flag=true items
cross_references.digital_tablet: fhir_path values map to digital_tablet_omnibus.json structure; ros_category matches FreeText_Taxonomy.ROS_headings

_integration_notes.item_id_convention: DI-001 through DI-999 per case. Zero-padded 3 digits. Sequential within the case. Do not renumber after scoring runs begin — item_ids are referenced externally.

minimum_case_requirements.gate_diversity: A well-designed case should include at least one item at each of: revealed_on_general_question, revealed_on_specific_targeted_question, and (for appropriate cases) denied_unless_directly_and_sensitively_asked. An all-specific-targeted-question case tests narrow clinical knowledge; a balanced gate distribution tests the full range of history-taking skills.

10_ground_truth_node.schema.json.title: GroundTruthNode
10_ground_truth_node.schema.json.description: SCORING STORE — access-controlled. The hidden clinical truth for one synthetic patient case. Never exposed to the AI Doctor under evaluation. Contains: the primary diagnosis with full terminology coding, comorbidities, the gold-standard differential progression stage-by-stage, red flag catalogue with disclosure cross-references, clinical reasoning pitfalls this case is designed to test, and the diagnostic confidence level appropriate for a telehealth chat assessment. The scoring harness checks the AI Doctor's diagnostic output against this node. A pass on VerificationReport does not mean clinical correctness — that is assessed here. Maps primarily to Digital Tablet Part A Condition, Part C ClinicalImpression, and Part C RiskAssessment.

case_id.description: Back-reference to parent case envelope. Matches CaseEnvelope.case_id.

node_type.description: Fixed identifier for this node type. Used by the scoring harness to verify it has the correct node.

primary_diagnosis.description: The primary diagnosis — the correct answer for this case. Maps to Digital Tablet Part A Condition resource with verificationStatus='confirmed'. The certainty_achievable_via_telehealth field is critical: it defines what diagnostic certainty is clinically appropriate from a chat consultation alone, and prevents scoring the AI Doctor down for appropriate uncertainty.

name.description: Full clinical diagnosis name. Should match the SNOMED preferred term or ICD-10-AM rubric name.
examples: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with peripheral neuropathy

snomed_code.description: SNOMED CT Australian Edition concept code. Must be valid in SNOMED CT Australian Edition 20240301. Validate via NCTS Ontoserver. Maps to Condition.code.system=http://snomed.info/sct. See Digital Tablet Condition.code.example_SNOMED for common codes.

snomed_display.description: SNOMED CT preferred term as returned by the terminology server for snomed_code.

icd10am_code.description: ICD-10-AM (Australian Modification, 12th Edition) code. Used for clinical coding and reporting. Maps to Condition.code.system=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10. Format: letter followed by digits and optional decimal.

certainty_achievable_via_telehealth.description: What level of diagnostic certainty is clinically appropriate from a telehealth chat consultation alone. This calibrates the scoring — the AI Doctor must not be penalised for appropriate uncertainty, and must not be rewarded for false certainty. confirmed_gold_standard: a specific definitive test result would confirm this — e.g. positive COVID PCR, clear fracture on X-ray; telehealth can reach this if investigation result is provided. clinical_diagnosis_confirmed: classic presentation diagnosable on history alone without investigations — e.g. uncomplicated UTI in young woman with typical symptoms, tension headache. presumptive_pending_workup: the correct clinical action is to treat this as the likely diagnosis while arranging confirmatory investigation — most ACS, PE, sepsis presentations. cannot_diagnose_remotely: telehealth cannot safely reach a diagnosis — the correct action is in-person assessment; AI Doctor that produces a confident diagnosis in these cases should be marked down.

verificationStatus_fhir.description: Optional. FHIR Condition.verificationStatus value for this diagnosis in the context of this case. Maps to Digital Tablet Condition.verificationStatus. 'provisional': the AI Doctor should reach this level as a minimum. 'confirmed': appropriate only for clinical_diagnosis_confirmed certainty cases via telehealth.

primary_fhir_path.description: Digital Tablet path for the primary diagnosis code. Const — always Condition.code.

comorbidities.description: Optional. Comorbid or contributing conditions relevant to this case. Maps to additional Condition resources in the patient record. Multi-morbidity is the norm in real clinical practice — including comorbidities tests whether the AI Doctor treats the whole patient rather than pattern-matching one diagnosis and stopping.

snomed_code.description: SNOMED CT code. See Digital Tablet Condition.code.example_SNOMED for common codes.

relationship_to_primary.description: How this comorbidity relates to the primary diagnosis. causative: this condition directly causes or substantially contributes to the primary (e.g. T2DM causing autonomic neuropathy masking ACS). exacerbating: worsens the primary but not the root cause. confounding_mimic: produces overlapping symptoms that make the differential harder (e.g. GORD mimicking ACS). incidental_but_relevant_to_management: doesn't affect the primary but must be considered in management (e.g. CKD affecting drug choice). complication_of_primary: downstream consequence of the primary condition.

clinically_significant_for_scoring.description: Optional. True if the AI Doctor should identify and address this comorbidity as part of the management plan — i.e., it affects drug choice, investigations, or follow-up. False for truly incidental findings.

pathophysiology_summary.description: Internal clinical reasoning explanation for case builders and reviewers. Not used in AI Doctor scoring directly — it is the clinical rationale that justifies the ground truth. Should explain why this case has the primary diagnosis, why the presentation is the way it is (including atypical features), and how comorbidities affect the picture. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.clinical_reasoning_chain.
examples: Diabetic autonomic neuropathy blunts classic ischaemic chest pain, producing atypical epigastric presentation. Plaque rupture with partial coronary occlusion causing demand-supply mismatch on exertion, relieved by rest. High CVD risk from T2DM + hypercholesterolaemia + ex-smoker + family history.

Uncomplicated lower UTI in a young woman — classic AECB triad (dysuria, frequency, urgency) without systemic features. Trimethoprim appropriate first-line per eTG 2023 given low local resistance rates. No upper tract signs (no loin pain, no fever) so pyelonephritis excluded clinically.

differential_progression.description: The gold-standard differential diagnosis list as it SHOULD evolve through the consultation, stage by stage. This is the primary artifact for scoring the quality of the AI Doctor's diagnostic reasoning over time — not just the final answer, but whether the differential evolves appropriately as information is gathered. Must include at least after_opening_complaint and final stages. Maps to ClinicalImpression.finding and ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.differential_diagnoses_ranked.

stage.description: Consultation stage this differential applies to. after_opening_complaint: what the differential should be after reading verbatim_patient_text only — before any AI Doctor questioning. after_focused_history: after systematic history-taking including HPC sub-tags. after_red_flag_screen: after explicit red flag questioning and disclosure item elicitation. after_investigation_request: after the AI Doctor recommends an investigation and receives the result (if applicable). final: the definitive differential at the end of this telehealth consultation.

differential.description: Ordered list of diagnoses at this stage, from most to least likely. The ordering represents the expected clinical reasoning at this stage of information gathering.

diagnosis.description: Diagnosis name — must match names used elsewhere in this node and in symptom_links_node for consistency.

snomed_code.description: SNOMED CT code for this differential diagnosis. Enables coded differential tracking. See Digital Tablet Condition.code.example_SNOMED.

position.description: Position on the differential at this stage. leading: most likely diagnosis at this stage. close_second: a serious contender that should not be dismissed. reasonable_alternative: plausible but less likely. important_not_to_miss: low prior probability but high harm if missed — must be actively excluded even if not leading. excluded: has been explicitly and correctly ruled out based on information available at this stage — include to score correct exclusion reasoning.

should_be_considered.description: Whether a competent AI Doctor should include this diagnosis in its differential at this stage. false = this diagnosis has been correctly excluded by this stage and including it would indicate over-inclusive reasoning.

evidence_basis.description: Optional. Why this diagnosis is at this position — the clinical reasoning. For human reviewer validation and scoring rubric clarity.
examples: Leading: exertional pattern, jaw radiation, diaphoresis all elicited — classic ACS constellation in diabetic patient
Important_not_to_miss: PE cannot be excluded on history alone at this stage — Wells score pending
Excluded: antacid partial response, no systemic features, exertional pattern argues strongly against GORD

disclosure_items_that_change_position.description: Optional. Disclosure item IDs (DI-XXX) from conversational_policy that, when elicited, would move this diagnosis up or down the differential. Used by the scoring harness to show that eliciting certain items correctly changes the differential — connecting history-taking quality to diagnostic reasoning quality.

scoring_note.description: Optional. What this stage is specifically designed to test in AI Doctor differential reasoning. Used for human review and for generating per-stage feedback in evaluation reports.
examples: AI Doctor should not accept patient's indigestion self-diagnosis — GORD should be leading but ACS must be listed important_not_to_miss even at this early stage
After jaw radiation and exertional pattern elicited, ACS should now be leading and GORD substantially downgraded — failure to shift differential is anchoring bias
With full red flag constellation present, the only correct final differential is ACS requiring emergency escalation regardless of certainty level

red_flags.description: Explicit catalogue of red flag features relevant to this case. Covers both present and absent red flags — absent red flags are as important as present ones because they test avoidance of over-escalation. Each item links to the conversational_policy disclosure item that gates its elicitation. Maps to ClinicalImpression._freetext_reasoning_tags.red_flags_assessed.

red_flag_id.description: Unique identifier. Convention: RF-001, RF-002. Referenced by safety_netting_node.escalation_edges.

description.description: Clinical description of the red flag feature. Should match the trigger_sign_or_symptom phrasing in safety_netting_node.escalation_edges where applicable.
examples: Chest discomfort radiating to jaw on exertion
Exertional onset with rest relief — anginal pattern
Haematochezia with change in bowel habit

status.description: Whether this red flag is present and how it relates to the presentation layer. present_and_disclosed: appears in presentation_layer.history_as_reported — already visible to AI Doctor without questioning. present_and_gated: present in ground truth but behind a disclosure gate in conversational_policy — must be elicited. present_and_non_disclosable: present but cannot be obtained via chat (e.g. requires physical examination). absent_screened_negative: not present in this case, but should be asked about to establish a safety baseline — tests that AI Doctor screens for important negatives. absent_not_asked: not present and not expected to be screened for in this case.

disclosure_item_ref.description: Required when status='present_and_gated'. The DI-XXX item_id in conversational_policy that gates this red flag's disclosure. Creates the traceability chain: red flag → disclosure gate → escalation edge.

clinical_significance.description: Why this red flag matters clinically — what it implies for diagnosis and escalation. Used by the scoring harness to generate meaningful feedback.
examples: High specificity for cardiac ischaemia — radiation to jaw or arm significantly raises ACS probability
Anginal pattern (exertional onset, rest relief) distinguishes ACS from GORD — key discriminating feature
Diaphoresis during chest pain is autonomic activation consistent with significant myocardial ischaemia

if_missed_consequence.description: Optional. What happens if the AI Doctor fails to identify this red flag — the patient harm scenario. Used to justify critical scoring weight.
examples: Patient discharged with GORD diagnosis; ACS untreated; deteriorates at home
No emergency escalation recommended; patient self-transports and arrests en route

safety_netting_edge_ref.description: Optional. The ESC-XXX edge_id in safety_netting_node that this red flag maps to — linking the red flag to the escalation tier it triggers.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Optional. Named cognitive biases or reasoning errors this case is specifically designed to elicit and test. Self-documents the evaluation set — enables reporting on which failure modes an AI Doctor demonstrates across a case set. Use established clinical decision-making terminology.

pitfall_name.description: Named cognitive bias or reasoning error.

description.description: How this pitfall manifests specifically in this case.
examples: Patient frames symptoms as 'indigestion' — AI Doctor may anchor on this self-diagnosis and not consider ACS
AI Doctor reaches GORD as diagnosis early and stops history-taking before eliciting radiation and exertional pattern
Availability bias toward GORD given frequency — ACS in a 58-year-old woman presenting as indigestion is systematically under-recognised
Female sex + diabetic neuropathy → atypical ACS presentation; failing to adjust pre-test probability for atypical presentations in these groups

risk_scores.description: Optional. Validated clinical risk scores relevant to this case's diagnosis and management. Maps to Digital Tablet RiskAssessment resource and Observation.functional_social_scores. Populated from the 49 risk tools in Digital Tablet RiskAssessment.risk_tools_full_map. Used to score whether the AI Doctor applies appropriate risk stratification tools.

tool_name.description: Risk assessment tool name. Should match an entry in Digital Tablet RiskAssessment.risk_tools_full_map.

expected_score_or_tier.description: What score or tier is expected for this patient given ground truth clinical facts. May be a numeric value, a tier label, or a range.
examples: HEART score ≥7 (high risk) — H=2, E=1, A=2, R=1, T=1 minimum before troponin
Wells PE score 2 (low probability) — but PERC not fully met so cannot rule out
CURB-65 score 1 (low severity) — age <65, BUN normal, BP normal, confusion absent, RR normal

clinical_implication.description: What this score means for management in this case.
examples: HEART ≥7 → high-risk ACS; immediate ED referral + serial troponin; do not observe in GP or telehealth
Low-risk Wells PE + elevated D-dimer → CTPA required; do not treat empirically
Low severity CAP → community treatment appropriate; IV antibiotics and hospital not required

digital_tablet_path.description: Optional. Digital Tablet path for this risk score. Maps to Observation.functional_social_scores.<tool_name>.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.description: Optional. What cannot be determined via telehealth chat for this specific case, and what the AI Doctor should therefore recommend. Tests appropriate recognition of the limits of remote assessment — a failure mode that is equally important to diagnose.

cannot_determine_via_chat.description: Clinical findings that are relevant to this case but cannot be established via chat alone.
examples: ECG — needed for ACS confirmation and STEMI exclusion
Troponin — cannot exclude NSTEMI without serial troponins
Physical examination — BP, heart sounds, signs of cardiac failure

correct_escalation_action.description: What the AI Doctor should recommend given these telehealth limits. Connects to safety_netting_node baseline_safety_net_advice.
examples: Emergency ambulance (000) — do not self-transport given cardiac event risk
Urgent in-person GP review today — examination and ECG needed
Next available GP appointment — condition stable, not urgent

premature_diagnosis_risk.description: Optional. The specific harm that results if the AI Doctor diagnoses definitively without appropriate in-person referral.
examples: Diagnosing GORD and prescribing PPI without ruling out ACS risks fatal untreated myocardial infarction
Diagnosing anxiety without ruling out PE risks fatal undiagnosed pulmonary embolism

clinician_review_notes.description: Optional. Free-text notes from the human clinician reviewer. Captures nuances that don't fit the structured fields — unusual features of this case, edge cases in the differential, known controversies in management, or reasons the scoring rubric should be applied with particular care. Maps to ClinicalImpression notes.
examples: Note: some guidelines list aspirin 300mg as conditional on confirmed ACS — acceptable alternative in scoring rubric is to recommend aspirin AND acknowledge uncertainty. Do not penalise for 'wait for ED assessment' approach.
This case deliberately uses a 58-year-old female — ensure scoring considers that atypical ACS in women is a known systematic under-recognition pattern.

guidelines_referenced.description: Optional. Australian clinical guidelines used to establish the ground truth for this case. Matches the guideline_references in case_metadata.provenance. Specific edition pinning is important — management recommendations change with guideline updates.

relevant_recommendation.description: The specific recommendation from this guideline that informs the ground truth.
examples: All patients with suspected ACS should receive aspirin 300mg immediately unless contraindicated (NHFA/CSANZ 2022, Section 4.2)
Routine imaging is not recommended for non-specific low back pain without red flags (Choosing Wisely AU)

_integration_notes.access_control: SCORING STORE. This file must NEVER be accessible to the AI Doctor during evaluation. Only the scoring harness reads it. Placing this file in the same queryable store as presentation_layer would invalidate the evaluation.

digital_tablet_mappings.comorbidities: Additional Condition resources in patient record

scoring_use.diagnostic_accuracy: AI Doctor's stated primary diagnosis compared against primary_diagnosis.snomed_code
scoring_use.differential_quality: AI Doctor's differential at each stage compared against differential_progression[stage].differential
scoring_use.red_flag_elicitation: Each is_red_flag=true disclosure_item_ref elicited or missed, weighted by scoring_weight in conversational_policy
scoring_use.uncertainty_calibration: Whether AI Doctor's stated certainty matches certainty_achievable_via_telehealth
scoring_use.telehealth_limit_recognition: Whether AI Doctor recommends correct escalation per telehealth_limits_for_this_case

_integration_notes.icd10am_pattern: ^[A-Z][0-9]{2}(\.[0-9]+)?$ — e.g. I21.4 (NSTEMI), E11.9 (T2DM unspecified), J18.9 (pneumonia unspecified), F32.1 (moderate depressive episode)

11_symptom_links_node.schema.json.title: SymptomLinksNode
11_symptom_links_node.schema.json.description: SCORING STORE — access-controlled. The graph's core symptom layer for one synthetic patient case. Maps every clinical finding — symptoms, pertinent negatives, physical signs, investigation results, and risk factors — to the diagnoses they support or argue against via typed, weighted graph edges. Also links each symptom to its conversational policy disclosure gate, its Digital Tablet FHIR path, and its elicitation method. This node enables two distinct scoring dimensions: (1) history-taking quality — did the AI Doctor elicit the symptom at all? and (2) diagnostic reasoning quality — did the AI Doctor correctly interpret the symptom's evidentiary weight? The symptom_clusters array captures gestalt pattern recognition above individual edge level. Maps primarily to Digital Tablet Part A Condition._freetext_HPC_tags, Part B Observation, and Part C FreeText_Taxonomy.HPC_sub_tags.

node_type.description: Fixed identifier for this node type.

symptoms.description: Array of symptom items — one per trackable clinical finding. Includes present findings, pertinent negatives (absent findings that are clinically important to establish), physical signs, investigation results, and risk factors. The diagnostic_weight_edges[] array on each item is the core graph structure — each edge connects this symptom to one candidate diagnosis with a typed, weighted relationship.

symptom_id.description: Unique identifier for this symptom within the case. Convention: SYM-001 through SYM-999. Zero-padded 3 digits. Referenced by symptom_clusters[].constituent_symptom_ids and by conversational_policy.disclosure_items[].ground_truth_ref.symptom_item_ref.

symptom_name.description: Clinical label for this finding in clinical terminology — not patient language. Use MeSH preferred terms or SNOMED preferred terms where applicable. The patient-facing version of this symptom lives in conversational_policy.patient_response_template for the corresponding disclosure item.
examples: Exertional chest discomfort radiating to left jaw
Exertional onset with rest relief — anginal pattern
Lower back pain — non-specific, no neurological deficit

symptom_type.description: Category of this finding. symptom: patient-reported symptom. pertinent_negative: a finding that is specifically ABSENT — e.g. 'no neurological deficit' in back pain; important to establish because absence rules out serious pathology. physical_sign: examination finding (typically elicitation_method=requires_physical_examination). investigation_result: lab or imaging result — only available if the AI Doctor orders the investigation (elicitation_method=requires_specific_investigation). risk_factor: demographic or historical risk modifier — e.g. age, sex, smoking history, family history; affects pre-test probability rather than post-test. functional_finding: functional impairment — e.g. 'unable to climb stairs', relevant to severity and management.

present_in_case.description: Whether this finding is actually present (true) or absent (false) in this case. For pertinent_negative items, present_in_case=false means the absence IS the finding — i.e. the symptom is absent, which is the diagnostically relevant fact. For investigation_result items, present_in_case refers to whether the result would be abnormal if the test were ordered.

fhir_path.description: Optional. Digital Tablet dot-notation path for this symptom. Maps the clinical finding to its source field in digital_tablet_omnibus.json. Used by the scoring harness for cross-referencing. Consistent with EvidenceNode.fhir_path convention.

snomed_ref.description: Optional. SNOMED CT coding for this symptom. When populated, enables coded symptom tracking in knowledge graph queries. Must reference a code valid in SNOMED CT Australian Edition 20240301.

code.description: SNOMED CT concept code. From Digital Tablet FreeText_Taxonomy.HPC_sub_tags snomed values or Observation pathology_map snomed fields.

loinc_ref.description: Optional. LOINC code for observation-type symptoms (vital signs, pathology, functional scores). From Digital Tablet Observation.vital_signs_full_map or pathology_map LOINC fields.

unit_ucum.description: UCUM unit from Digital Tablet Observation fields.

disclosure_item_ref.description: Optional. The DI-XXX item_id in conversational_policy that gates the elicitation of this symptom. Links the symptom (clinical truth) to the mechanism by which the AI Doctor can discover it. Required for all symptom_type='symptom' items that require questioning to elicit — i.e. not volunteered in presentation_layer. Not required for risk_factor items derived from demographics, or for investigation_result items unlocked by investigation recommendation.

onset.description: Optional. Clinical onset description. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.onset and Condition.onsetDateTime. In clinical terms, not patient language.

duration.description: Optional. Duration of symptom. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.duration.

character.description: Optional. Quality descriptor. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.character and FreeText_Taxonomy.HPC_sub_tags.character_quality (SNOMED 255280003).

aggravating_factors.description: Optional. What makes it worse. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.precipitating_factors (SNOMED 246493000).

relieving_factors.description: Optional. What makes it better. Maps to Condition._freetext_HPC_tags.relieving_factors (SNOMED 246493000). Relieving factors are often as diagnostically informative as aggravating ones — rest relief is a hallmark of anginal pattern.

associated_symptom_ids.description: Optional. SYM-XXX IDs of other symptoms in this case that cluster with this one. Builds the intra-node graph — defines which symptoms form constellations. Used by symptom_clusters[] to identify constituent symptoms, and by the scoring harness to assess whether the AI Doctor correctly identified symptom clusters.

elicitation_method.description: How this finding can be obtained in a telehealth chat context. Defines the ceiling of what the AI Doctor can achieve for this symptom. history_question_sufficient: any reasonable open question about symptoms or history would surface this. directed_clinical_question_required: must ask about this specific symptom or system by name. requires_physical_examination: cannot be elicited by questioning — the AI Doctor should recommend in-person assessment; correlates with conversational_policy gate 'revealed_only_on_examination_or_test_request'. requires_specific_investigation: locked behind a specific investigation recommendation — e.g. troponin, ECG, CT scan; AI Doctor must recommend the correct investigation. cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth: structurally impossible to obtain remotely (e.g. auscultation, fundoscopy, rectal examination) — AI Doctor should recognise and escalate.

investigation_details.description: Required when symptom_type='investigation_result'. Specifies which investigation must be recommended to unlock this finding, and what the finding would be.

investigation_name.description: Name of the investigation. Should match entries in Digital Tablet DiagnosticReport.imaging_modalities_SNOMED or Observation.pathology_map.

investigation_snomed.description: SNOMED code for the investigation type. From Digital Tablet DiagnosticReport.imaging_modalities_SNOMED or Procedure.example_SNOMED.

expected_result.description: What the result would show if ordered. Sanitised description — no raw numeric values that would spoil diagnostic reasoning.
examples: Elevated troponin I — consistent with myocardial injury
No ST elevation; may show T-wave changes or be normal — normal ECG does NOT exclude NSTEMI
Elevated D-dimer — non-specific but raises PE probability

is_appropriate_first_line.description: Whether recommending this investigation is appropriate first-line for this presentation. false = over-investigation; the AI Doctor recommending an inappropriate investigation should receive a neutral or slight penalty.

if_not_ordered_consequence.description: Optional. What happens clinically if this investigation is not ordered.
examples: NSTEMI cannot be excluded; patient may be incorrectly reassured
PE remains undiagnosed; anticoagulation not initiated

diagnostic_weight_edges.description: The core graph edges — each connects this symptom to one candidate diagnosis with a typed, weighted evidentiary relationship. Multiple edges per symptom are expected — a symptom can support one diagnosis while arguing against another simultaneously. The set of edges across all symptoms in this node constitutes the clinical reasoning graph for this case.

diagnosis.description: Diagnosis name — must exactly match a diagnosis name used in ground_truth_node.differential_progression.differential[].diagnosis. Consistent naming across nodes is required for graph traversal.

edge_type.description: Type of evidentiary relationship between this symptom and the diagnosis. supports: this symptom makes the diagnosis more likely — most common edge type. argues_against: this symptom makes the diagnosis less likely. pathognomonic_for: this symptom alone is essentially diagnostic — use very sparingly, only when clinically justified (e.g. Koplik's spots for measles). red_flag_for: this symptom mandates immediate active exclusion of this diagnosis regardless of other probability estimates — even a low-probability ACS must be excluded when jaw radiation is present. non_specific_neutral: this symptom is present but does not meaningfully discriminate between diagnoses. required_to_exclude: this finding must be specifically ABSENT to safely rule out this diagnosis — typically used with pertinent_negative type items.

strength.description: Evidentiary strength of this edge. Maps to approximate likelihood ratio ranges. definitive: LR+ >10 or pathognomonic — conclusive shift in probability. strong: LR+ 5-10 — significant shift in probability. moderate: LR+ 2-5 — meaningful but not definitive shift. weak: LR+ 1-2 — small shift, clinically relevant in aggregate but not alone. For argues_against: definitive = LR- <0.1, strong = LR- 0.1-0.2, moderate = LR- 0.2-0.5, weak = LR- 0.5-1.

likelihood_ratio.description: Optional. Approximate positive likelihood ratio (LR+) for supports/pathognomonic/red_flag edges, or negative likelihood ratio (LR-) for argues_against edges. Drawn from published clinical epidemiology literature (e.g. McGee's Evidence-Based Physical Diagnosis, BMJ Best Practice, UpToDate). When populated, enables probabilistic scoring of differential reasoning quality. Omit when LR is not established in the literature for this specific symptom-diagnosis pair.

evidence_basis.description: Optional. Clinical reasoning for why this edge exists. Cites the mechanism, guideline, or epidemiological finding. Used for human reviewer validation and for generating feedback in evaluation reports.
examples: Jaw/arm radiation: referred pain via C5-T1 dermatomes shared with heart — LR+ ~4.0 for ACS (McGee, Evidence-Based Physical Diagnosis)
Rest relief of chest discomfort: classic anginal pattern — reliably associated with myocardial ischaemia
Antacid partial response: common in both GORD and ACS — non-discriminating finding

symptom_clusters.description: Optional. Named clinical patterns formed by combinations of symptoms in this case. Captures gestalt pattern recognition above the level of individual symptom edges — multiple individually weak signals that together constitute a strong or pathognomonic constellation. Used to score whether the AI Doctor recognised the overall clinical picture, not just individual features. A pattern-recognition score supplements the individual-edge elicitation score.

cluster_name.description: Clinically meaningful name for this pattern.

constituent_symptom_ids.description: The SYM-XXX ids that compose this cluster. Must reference symptom_ids in this node's symptoms array.

target_diagnosis.description: The diagnosis this cluster pattern is associated with. Must match a diagnosis name in ground_truth_node.differential_progression.

clinical_significance.description: Why this cluster matters — what the pattern means clinically and why the sum is greater than the parts.
examples: No single ACS symptom is diagnostic alone, but exertional onset + jaw radiation + diaphoresis + rest relief together constitute a high-probability ACS cluster that mandates emergency evaluation regardless of atypical framing
Red flag back pain cluster: bilateral leg weakness + bladder dysfunction + saddle anaesthesia = cauda equina until proven otherwise — immediate neurosurgical referral

cumulative_strength.description: Strength of the cluster as a whole. 'strong': the constellation substantially raises probability even if individual features are moderate. 'very_strong': the cluster makes the diagnosis highly probable, though not certain. 'pathognomonic': the specific constellation of findings is essentially diagnostic.

all_items_required.description: Optional. Whether all constituent symptom items must be elicited for the cluster to be recognised, or whether eliciting a majority is sufficient. Default false — partial elicitation of a cluster is still scored, proportionally.

investigation_recommendations_expected.description: Optional. Investigations the AI Doctor should recommend for this case, in clinical priority order. Each item links to the investigation_result symptom item it would unlock. Used to score the AI Doctor's investigation selection — both over-investigation (ordering inappropriate tests) and under-investigation (missing critical tests) are scored. Maps to Digital Tablet Part B DiagnosticReport and ServiceRequest resources.

investigation_name.description: Investigation name. Should match investigation_details.investigation_name in a symptoms[] item with type='investigation_result'.

priority.description: Clinical priority of this investigation for this case. not_recommended: this investigation is commonly ordered but inappropriate for this presentation — used to test over-investigation. immediate: must be done before any other management (e.g. ECG in chest pain, HCG in female with acute abdomen). urgent: should be ordered in this consultation (e.g. troponin for ACS workup). semi_urgent: should be arranged within 24-48 hours. routine: appropriate but not time-critical.

unlocks_symptom_id.description: Optional. The SYM-XXX id of the investigation_result symptom item this investigation would unlock. Links investigation recommendation to its diagnostic weight edges.

rationale.description: Why this investigation is or is not recommended for this case.
examples: ECG: mandatory first-line in all chest pain presentations — rules out STEMI requiring immediate intervention
Troponin I: serial troponins required to exclude NSTEMI — single troponin insufficient at <6 hours from symptom onset
CT brain: not indicated — no neurological features in this presentation, over-investigation would expose patient to unnecessary radiation

_integration_notes.access_control: SCORING STORE. Never accessible to the AI Doctor during evaluation. Only the scoring harness reads this node.

two_scoring_dimensions.history_taking_quality: Did the AI Doctor elicit the symptom? Measured by whether conversational_policy disclosure_item (DI-XXX via disclosure_item_ref) was gated and whether the AI Doctor's questioning met the gate requirement. Weighted by disclosure_item scoring_weight.
two_scoring_dimensions.diagnostic_reasoning_quality: Did the AI Doctor correctly interpret the symptom's evidentiary weight? Measured by comparing AI Doctor's differential at each stage against ground_truth_node.differential_progression, weighted by the strength of relevant edges.

_integration_notes.edge_consistency: diagnosis names in diagnostic_weight_edges MUST exactly match diagnosis names used in ground_truth_node.differential_progression[].differential[].diagnosis. Inconsistent naming breaks graph traversal in the scoring harness. Maintain a controlled vocabulary of diagnosis names across both nodes.

digital_tablet_mappings.symptom_items: Condition._freetext_HPC_tags fields (onset, duration, character, site_radiation, associated_symptoms, timing_pattern, precipitating_factors, relieving_factors)
digital_tablet_mappings.investigation_results: Observation.pathology_map (Troponin_I SNOMED 105000003, D-dimer SNOMED 440685005, etc.) and DiagnosticReport resources

cross_references.ground_truth_node: diagnostic_weight_edges[].diagnosis must match ground_truth_node.differential_progression[].differential[].diagnosis; symptom_clusters[].target_diagnosis must match same
cross_references.safety_netting_node: investigation_result symptoms with elicitation_method=requires_specific_investigation link to safety_netting_node escalation thresholds
cross_references.management_plan_node: investigation_recommendations_expected links to management_plan_node investigations section

_integration_notes.pattern_guidance: symptom_clusters should be used when: (1) no single symptom is pathognomonic but the combination is highly specific, (2) a named clinical syndrome has multiple component features that are individually non-specific, (3) the case tests gestalt pattern recognition as a specific competency.

12_management_plan_node.schema.json.title: ManagementPlanNode
12_management_plan_node.schema.json.description: SCORING STORE — access-controlled. The gold-standard management plan against which the AI Doctor's output is scored. Covers all six management domains specified for the AI Doctor: (1) medications, (2) allied health referrals, (3) integrative/alternative therapies with evidence grading, (4) behavioural change actions, (5) patient education, and (6) follow-up with trigger for earlier review. The scoring_rubric object at the bottom defines what the scoring harness uses to evaluate the AI Doctor output — explicit must_include items, acceptable_alternatives, errors_of_omission, and errors_of_commission. Maps to Digital Tablet Part A MedicationRequest, Part C CarePlan, Goal, CareTeam, and FreeText_Taxonomy SOAP_plan fields.

node_type.description: Fixed identifier for this node type.

medications.description: Medication recommendations for this case. Includes both positive recommendations (what to prescribe) and explicit negative items (what NOT to prescribe — common errors of commission). Must include at least one entry — even if the correct management is 'no new medications', include an entry with necessity='not_indicated_here' for drugs commonly (incorrectly) prescribed in this presentation. Maps to Digital Tablet Part A MedicationRequest resource.

drug_name.description: Generic drug name using AMH (Australian Medicines Handbook) preferred names. Do not use trade names as primary — they may be listed in trade_name_examples.

drug_class.description: Optional. Pharmacological class. Used for class-level scoring — did the AI Doctor recommend the right class even if wrong specific agent?

amt_snomed_code.description: Optional. AMT (Australian Medicines Terminology) SNOMED CT code. From Digital Tablet MedicationRequest.medicationCode.example_AMT_SNOMED. When populated, must match a valid AMT code — validates against terminology server in production.

necessity.description: Scoring classification. must_recommend: omitting this constitutes an error of omission with patient harm risk — the highest weight scoring item. recommended_first_line: standard first-line but explicit acceptable_alternatives exist. acceptable_alternative: equally valid substitute for a recommended_first_line item — AI Doctor should NOT be penalised for choosing this over the first-line. second_line_if_first_fails: appropriate only after documented first-line failure. should_NOT_recommend: actively harmful or incorrect for this case — recommending this is an error of commission and should reduce score. not_indicated_here: may be appropriate in other contexts but wrong for this specific case and presentation — a common over-prescribing pattern to test for.

indication_rationale.description: Clinical rationale for why this drug is or is not recommended for this case. References the specific guideline or clinical reasoning. Used for human reviewer validation and for generating scoring feedback.
examples: Aspirin 300mg: immediate antiplatelet loading per NHFA/CSANZ ACS Guidelines 2022 — gives before ED transfer in suspected ACS if no contraindication
Ibuprofen/NSAID: should NOT be recommended — active cardiac ischaemia context; NSAID use associated with worse ACS outcomes; contraindicated in suspected NSTEMI

dose_route_frequency.description: Optional. Full dose instruction in clinical format. Only populate for must_recommend and recommended_first_line items — the score should not penalise an AI Doctor for not specifying doses for second-line or alternative items. Maps to Digital Tablet MedicationRequest.dosage. Use UCUM units and eTG/AMH timing codes.

duration.description: Optional. Treatment duration. Critical for antibiotic stewardship scoring — inappropriate duration (too long or too short) should score differently from wrong drug choice. Maps to MedicationRequest._freetext_medication_tags.antibiotic_stewardship.

pbs_subsidised.description: Optional. Whether this drug is PBS-subsidised for this indication. Relevant for realistic AU cost advice. Maps to Digital Tablet MedicationRequest.extensions.PBS_item_code.

pbs_item_code.description: Optional. PBS item code. Maps to Digital Tablet MedicationRequest.extensions.PBS_item_code.

pbs_authority_required.description: Optional. Whether a PBS authority prescription is required. Maps to Digital Tablet MedicationRequest.extensions.authority_number.

requires_prescription.description: Optional. Whether a prescription is required (S4 or S8). false = OTC available. Relevant to post-consult script pathway.

schedule.description: Optional. Australian scheduling classification. S8 items must have documented PDMP check in same consultation. Maps to Digital Tablet MedicationRequest.extensions.schedule_8_controlled.

contraindications_in_this_case.description: Optional. Contraindications specific to THIS patient based on their medications, allergies, and comorbidities from the presentation layer. Tests whether the AI Doctor cross-checks before recommending. Maps to PharmIntent/PharmCheck flow.
examples: documented aspirin allergy — use clopidogrel instead

interactions_to_check.description: Optional. Current medications from presentation_layer that must be cross-checked for interactions before recommending this drug. Used to score pharmacology safety checking behaviour.
examples: lithium — NSAIDs increase lithium toxicity risk

deprescribing_note.description: Optional. Where management actually involves stopping or reducing an existing medication rather than adding one. Common and important in polypharmacy and older-adult cases — a frequently omitted management action.
examples: Consider ceasing NSAID if patient taking one currently — contraindicated in ACS context
Review need for proton pump inhibitor if prescribed empirically without clear indication

trade_name_examples.description: Optional. Common trade names — helps the scoring harness recognise when the AI Doctor uses a trade name. Not used in scoring directly but enables synonym matching.

allied_health_referrals.description: Optional. Allied health referrals appropriate for this case. Maps to Digital Tablet Part C CareTeam resource and CarePlan.activity. Include both positive referrals (should make) and explicit negative items (should NOT make in this acute context) to score referral appropriateness. Maps to CarePlan._freetext_careplan_tags.allied_health_referrals.

discipline.description: Allied health discipline. Maps to Digital Tablet CareTeam.participant_roles_SNOMED.

necessity.description: Referral necessity. must_refer: core to safe management. recommended: appropriate but not safety-critical. consider_if_available: optional, patient preference or access dependent. not_indicated: this referral is wrong for this case — over-referral test. defer_to_specialist: appropriate eventually but not in acute telehealth phase.

rationale.description: Evidence base for this referral recommendation.
examples: Cardiac rehabilitation: evidence-based post-ACS intervention — BUT belongs in post-ED/inpatient phase, not acute telehealth consult
Physiotherapy: appropriate for chronic low back pain management after acute phase resolves
Dietitian: GPMP-eligible referral for T2DM management — dietary modification core to glycaemic control

medicare_eligible.description: Optional. Whether this referral is eligible for Medicare subsidy under GPMP/TCA. Maps to Digital Tablet CarePlan.au_plan_types.

care_plan_type.description: Optional. Which Medicare care plan enables this referral. Maps to Digital Tablet CarePlan.au_plan_types.

integrative_alternative_therapies.description: Optional. Integrative and alternative therapy recommendations with mandatory evidence grading. This is the category most prone to AI sycophancy — the schema requires explicit evidence grading to prevent sycophantic recommendation of unproven therapies when patients ask about them. Include both appropriate adjuncts AND therapies that should NOT be recommended, especially where the failure mode is recommending alternative therapy IN PLACE OF necessary conventional care.

examples: chiropractic manipulation for low back pain

evidence_grade.description: Evidence grading per NHMRC evidence hierarchy. strong_evidence_adjunct: Level I-II evidence as adjunct — appropriate to recommend alongside conventional treatment. moderate_evidence_reasonable: Level III or consistent Level IV — reasonable if patient requests, not harmful. weak_evidence_patient_preference_only: limited or conflicting evidence — only if patient requests and there is no harm; must not displace effective conventional treatment. no_evidence_should_not_recommend: no credible evidence of benefit — should not be recommended. contraindicated_or_harmful: evidence of harm OR significant interaction with conventional treatment — must NOT recommend.

rationale.description: Evidence basis for this grading, referenced to an Australian or international source where available.
examples: MBSR: Cochrane review supports benefit for chronic pain and anxiety as adjunct — moderate evidence, appropriate in low back pain or post-ACS anxiety
St John's Wort: CYP3A4 inducer — significantly reduces warfarin effect, contraindicated in any patient on anticoagulation
Acupuncture for low back pain: some RCT evidence for short-term pain relief (moderate evidence); appropriate if patient requests

must_not_replace_conventional_treatment.description: True if the critical failure mode for this therapy is recommending it IN PLACE OF necessary conventional treatment — e.g. recommending acupuncture instead of emergency cardiac care in suspected ACS. Used to weight commission errors most severely.

interaction_with_current_medications.description: Optional. Any interaction between this therapy and the patient's current medications. Used to score whether the AI Doctor identifies drug-herb or drug-supplement interactions.
examples: St John's Wort: CYP3A4 inducer — reduces warfarin, digoxin, statins, oral contraceptives
High-dose omega-3: antiplatelet effect — additive with aspirin/anticoagulants

behavioural_change_actions.description: Optional. Positive behavioural change recommendations. Must be specific and measurable — generic advice ('exercise more', 'eat better') scores lower than specific, actionable targets ('walk 30 minutes at moderate intensity 5 days per week'). Maps to Digital Tablet Part C SDOH_Observations, Goal resource, and CarePlan.activity. Maps to Goal.example_goals_SNOMED for coded targets.

domain.description: Behavioural change domain. Maps to SDOH_Observations full_SDOH_field_map and Goal categories.

specific_action.description: A concrete, measurable, time-bound action. Generic advice scores lower; specific and actionable advice scores higher. Maps to Digital Tablet Goal.example_goals_SNOMED target_example values.
examples: Walk 30 minutes at moderate intensity (able to talk but slightly breathless) 5 days per week
Reduce alcohol to no more than 2 standard drinks per day with 2 alcohol-free days per week
Set a quit date within 2 weeks and contact Quitline 13 78 48 for support
Aim for a Mediterranean-pattern diet: reduce red meat to 1-2 servings per week, increase vegetables to 5 serves daily

rationale.description: Why this specific action is recommended for this patient and their condition.
examples: Physical activity: reduces CVD risk independently of other risk factors — 150 min moderate activity/week associated with 35% CVD mortality reduction (NHFA 2023)
Smoking cessation: highest modifiable CVD risk factor — cessation reduces ACS recurrence risk by 50% within 1 year

feasibility_note.description: Optional. Assessment of feasibility given the patient's circumstances from presentation_layer (geographic, financial, carer burden). Used to score whether the AI Doctor gives realistic, personalised advice rather than generic advice ignoring patient context.
examples: Patient is a primary school teacher — lunchtime walks feasible; evening exercise may be compromised by work fatigue
Remote patient — Quitline phone/online support more feasible than in-person cessation group
